evals.prompt: I just ran the timeline extraction on a slip opinion and got a JSON file. Can you render it as a chart I can show my partners tomorrow? Pick the best chart style for an appellate prep meeting.
evals.expected_output: Renders the JSON to a self-contained HTML file. Should pick dual-track style for appellate framing (matches the rationale in the user prompt). Reports the style choice and any validation warnings.

evals.prompt: Here's a partial event list from a chronology I built in Excel. Can you turn it into a timeline chart even though I don't have all the schema fields? I just have label, date, and which party did it.
evals.expected_output: Handles minimal input gracefully. Auto-derives lanes from parties, defaults missing fields, renders the chart, and reports what was filled in.

evals.prompt: I have the extraction JSON for a multi-defendant construction case. Show me both a swimlane view (with separate lanes per defendant) and a dual-track view (facts vs procedure) so I can decide which to use in the brief.
evals.expected_output: Renders BOTH styles to separate HTML files. Reports differences in what each emphasizes.
